notes: Multi-use positioning (cooking + skincare + wellness) is confusing for US consumers - would need to pick one lane
Honey subscription boxes already exist (Bee Raw, etc.) - hard to differentiate without premium pricing that limits market
Validated the fresh-mix version instead - pre-mixed coffee scrubs already exist and are inferior
Pomegranate wellness trend has peaked, hard to compete with established pomegranate juice brands
Roller ball format requires precise filling equipment, margins not strong enough to justify complexity
Carob has a reputation problem in US (1970s health food), hard to overcome taste perception
Hair rinse is very niche use case, would need broader positioning as regular tea first
